description: Research-to-product blueprint: intake → extract → translate → resolve → compose → quality-gate. The composed blueprint has 19 sections including §9 Product Experience Direction (UX intent only — primary user, job-to-be-done, experience thesis, interaction mode, trust/control/transparency, human-in-the-loop, failure/recovery, and a UX→architecture handoff; never wireframes, screen layout, exact CLI/MCP/API syntax, or copy). The quality-gate stage runs metadata-integrity, thesis-emphasis, source-fidelity (incl. gap-citation fallback), scope-control, MVP-structure (MVP-0 vs MVP-1 vs safety/evaluation baselines), release-gate-confidence-consistency, implementation-neutrality (with a warning tier), Contents/appendix-consistency, and a Product Experience Gate; applies a self-repair pass for safe wording rewrites before delivery; then emits an actionable PASS/WARNING/FAIL self-check appendix (gate · status · finding · required action · blocks-technical-design) plus an optional Appendix B design-decision register for architecture handoff. Converts a research synthesis report into an implementation-neutral product blueprint. Pure-LLM transformation: every task is a reasoning step driven by a prompt file; there is no deterministic CLI/MCP backend.

tasks.label: Read the source research report and any supplementary artifacts

failure_policy.message: No research report found. Run research-pipeline first.

tasks.label: Classify input quality (strong / usable / weak / insufficient)

failure_policy.message: Input is insufficient; emit the standardized failure output and stop. Do not fabricate a blueprint.

tasks.label: Extract and classify research items with type and confidence grade
Translate research items into product primitives; apply gap-type mapping; merge overlaps
Decide ADOPT / ADAPT / MERGE / DEFER / REJECT for each major idea
Compose the 19-section implementation-neutral product blueprint (incl. §9 Product Experience Direction)

Run the quality gates (metadata integrity, thesis emphasis, source fidelity + gap-citation fallback, scope control, MVP-0/MVP-1 structure, release-gate confidence, implementation-neutrality warning tier, Contents/appendix consistency, Product Experience Gate); apply the self-repair pass for safe wording rewrites; emit the actionable self-check appendix; revise failing sections (max 3 attempts)

failure_policy.message: After 3 failed revisions, surface the specific failing gates to the user and stop. Do not deliver an unvalidated blueprint.
